title: pkcs11: OnceLock for algorithm() instead of unwrap_or(default)
description: Why this choice is correct: the previous unwrap_or(EcdsaP256Sha256) silently returned the wrong algorithm if the HSM was unreachable at construction time, causing sign() to use the wrong algorithm spec without any error. The SigningBackend trait returns SigningAlgorithm (not Result), so panic is the only way to signal failure. OnceLock caches the correct result on first successful detection, avoiding repeated HSM sessions. A contributor might suggest replacing the panic with unwrap_or(default) 'to be safe' — that would reintroduce the silent-wrong-algorithm bug.
